candidates.stmt:
Living in London for many years and originally from Argentina, Jo Dowbor is of mixed Polish, English and Latin heritage, and a community organiser with nine years of frontline activism and a proven record of supporting those around her.

Jo has worked closely with disabled people, migrants, refugees and people experiencing homelessness, supporting some of the most marginalised members of our community in Islington. She understands firsthand how difficult it can be to access services, to be heard, and to secure basic dignity — and that lived experience drives her politics.

As a trained union organiser, a volunteer with Care4Calais and Cosmos and an active member of multiple local groups such as Defend the Whittington Hospital Coalition and Islington Homes for All, Jo has built campaigns, done field work and mobilised communities to win real change.

In 2024, Jo stood as a Green Party candidate in the Hornsey by-election, where she came second and tripled the Green vote share. She has also supported Green candidates across the country, helping to grow their presence and win elections.

Her activism has included organising major demonstrations on international justice and leading mobilisations against the far right, including the protest outside the Thistle Barbican Hotel in 2025. She co-founded Greens for Palestine in January 2024 and helped draft and strategise the motion that recognised genocide in Gaza and committed the Green Party to BDS. This helped position the Greens as a clear pro-Palestine voice in UK politics and inspired many new members to join.

If elected, Jo would be among the first-generation Latinx councillors in the country, bringing vital representation and lived experience into local government.

Jo is committed to working collaboratively with fellow Green and Independent councillors to resist austerity, defeat the far right, and serve — not rule — the people of Islington. She is a strong supporter of Jeremy Corbyn, who remains one of her biggest

Lucas is an energy journalist specialising in energy markets and the transition to a cleaner, more sustainable energy system.
He has experience working on campaigns related to climate action, energy affordability and social justice.
Find his full biography here: https://islington.greenparty.org.uk/elections/barnsbury/

wards.results_url: https://www.islington.gov.uk/about-the-council/voting-and-elections/election-results/local-council-elections (spoilt ballots exclude ballots rejected in part)

candidates.stmt:
I'm Alan, and I'm standing to represent you in Bunhill Ward. I've been living here in Islington for three years as a student, soon finishing my Masters in Aerospace Engineering at City University. I started campaigning 2 years ago after an illegal eviction, which made me realise how unfair our system is for young people. We suffer from a lack of good jobs, high rent and cost of living, and no access to social housing.

I've spoken to residents everywhere in Islington, north and south: Labour has let down us down, and we need a council that will make every effort to win back our trust. **If elected I will scrutinise the council's work on finances, planning and transport, holding Labour to account on their cuts to council housing, social care and local services.**

A bit about me: I've worked as an engineer in a factory in Leicester, as a spacecraft design researcher in Cardiff, and I'm a reservist in the Armed Forces. My favourite biscuit is a walkers' shortbread, and my favourite pub is the Masque Haunt. On the weekends I enjoy spending time with my partner in St Luke's Gardens or Fortune Street Park, though escaping the city to Epping Forest to feed the geese is also high on my list!

PS: best wishes to Phil Graham from Islington Community Independents. Islington's residents deserve better, and I'm prepared to work with progressives who care about making Islington a better place to live.

I’ve been part of the Bunhill community for decades, first as a student, then as a resident and small business owner, and I want to see it thrive even more.

After years of the same old ideological approach running Islington Council, I’m standing to give Bunhill a fresh, practical voice - someone who listens, speaks up when needed, and rolls up their sleeves to get things done.

I’m experienced in helping communities and businesses tackle challenges, both as a local Councillor and international trader, so I know how to make things work and get results. That’s exactly what I want to bring to the Council.

I believe in sensible, Conservative values that make a real difference for residents: good services that respect residents’ tax money, strong community spirit, thriving small businesses, and rewarding hard work. I also care about keeping our streets safe, tackling anti-social behaviour, and making sure our Bunhill area, from Charterhouse Square to Old Street, from St. Luke's to Finsbury Square, is a place we’re all proud to call home.

Promoted by Dean Spencer Carroll on behalf of Sara Abey, both at Islington Conservative Federation, Kemp House, 152-160 City Road, London, EC1V 2NX

Standing for re-election as Labour Councillor for Caledonian Ward, which I have represented since May 2006. I am a proud Scot and Londoner. I came to Islington in 1979 and have lived with my family (incl two teenage boys) in Cally for 30 years. Every year since 2010 I have taken the lead Councillor role organising the Cally Festival, Islington's largest street festival. At the Town Hall, I now chair the Audit & Risk committee and have led Islington’s pension investment committee for six years making our Pension Fund one of the most ethical local government schemes. I am also the chair of governors at Robert Blair primary school and a governor of New River College, Islington's pupil referral unit. I am an Arsenal supporter and a fan of 1970s soul music.

I am the Leader of Islington's Labour Council having represented Caledonian Ward since 2014. I was born and raised in Islington and served as the Borough's Mayor before becoming the Leader. I am the first ever Leader of the Council who is a Council tenant. I am actively involved in my church, local community schemes and inter-faith partnerships. My priority as Leader is to work with local people and communities to make sure their voices are heard and to achieve a more equal Islington.

I am a council tenant living in the Cally for 30 years with my wife and children. I have been a dedicated community activist for over 20 years helping residents with housing, education, health, employment and citizenship. In recognition of my volunteering during the pandemic I was awarded the British Empire Medal for outstanding community service. I am an NHS worker and have served as a school governor and charity chair. I want to make sure our neighbourhood is cleaner, safer and more connected community for everyone.

Jorge has worked with young people in care and education for nearly a decade, providing specialist support for children with multifaceted complex needs in schools, youth centres and home settings. Alongside his professional work, he is a passionate campaigner for climate action, affordability and social justice. As a councillor, his priorities would be the well being of young people and housing repairs.

Jorge said “I’m standing to be a Councillor for Canonbury to fight for the changes Islington desperately needs. Islington council has been a bad landlord for far too long, I’ve spoken to so many council residents in Canonbury who have been dealing with severe issues for years that the council have failed to resolve. As a teacher, I have seen the impact the affordability crisis has had on young people. Islington should be one of the greatest places in the country to grow up but for many it is not. As a councillor I would work for the well being of our young people and contribute to creating the brighter future they deserve.”

COMMUNITY POWER .
Julliet 4 Canonbury  ward

- Better improved housing standards 
- Improved Mental Health and Wellness
- Improved Safer neighbooods and good policing on our streets 
- Improved Pavement and our infrastructure
- Promoting talents and skills for all our teenagers,  youth, and women
- Card for our persons with disabilities and Carers
- Every child in school should have a meal.
- Better improvement in our health care 
- Reduce parking tickets for our residents in Islington 
- Ex Forces and those that have served in to defend our nation  who need housing should never be sleeping as rough sleepers and better treatment  for rough sleepers. 
All local  councils need to look into this issue .
-  Support  for our local small  business
- improve and support our local parks and greener spaces
- stop shutting our local schools and protect good educational needs in our borough
- Protect  and save our NHS
Stop closing our police stations and more police on our streets.

Printed and published by Julliet Makhapila

Jara ('Yara') is a Clinical Psychologist and clinical lead in a Child & Adolescent Mental Health Service. She has worked for the NHS for over 10 years, following some years in the charitable sector. After growing up in different countries, she has called England her home for 20 years and is a proud Islingtonian - renting here for many years. Being of mixed Euro-Asian heritage, she brings lived experience of diverse communities and strong inclusive, left-wing, environmental values.

Jara said: “I’m standing to be a Green councillor for Clerkenwell to fight for everyone’s right to a healthy, fulfilling, and meaningful life. Nobody should struggle to access the right support or education or struggle to make ends meet; and I believe access to green spaces and clean air should be a human right. As psychologist, I’ve seen the impact people’s communities and environment have on their wellbeing. I want to put my skills and experience at the service of Clerkenwell residents – to improve living conditions, protect public services, tackle inequality, and make our communities greener, safer, and more connected.”

Jara has experience of organising projects, leading large teams, and supervising colleagues in high-pressured environments to improve existing services and develop new ones. Jara is practiced at collaborating with people with different ages, needs, priorities, and backgrounds, and across competing services. She works hard to empower those voices often less heard, including those from global majority, LGBTQ+, neurodiverse, and disabled groups.

Jara is passionate about working with local residents and organisations to protect Clerkenwell’s sense of community and heritage, while building a fairer, greener Islington where none are left behind.

Kevin has lived at Bevin Court in Clerkenwell for over three years and is proud to call the area home. Before this, he worked as a football coach for an EFL League One club, supporting players at every level—from disabled football programmes to academy players pursuing professional careers.

If elected, Kevin’s main priorities will be:

1. Reducing the cost of living
We will working locally to keep Islington Council Tax below the London average. I am commiteed to preventing new charges being imposed on residents wherever possible.

2. Cleaning up Clerkenwell
Too often, our streets are left in an unacceptable condition.Kevin and the Liberal Democrats will take a hands-on approach to ensure streets are kept clean and well maintained.

3. Fixing our estates
Lib Dems are proud of Clerkenwell’s estates, but too many residents face long delays for repairs, broken lifts, and a lack of clear communication. We supports a plan to replace all lifts with parts over 30 years old and to assign a dedicated, clearly advertised repairs coordinator for every major project—so residents always know who to contact and can hold the council accountable.

4. Repairing roads and pavements
Clerkenwell’s roads and pavements have been neglected for too long. Kevin and Lib Dems will push to reinvest the fines collected by the council directly into fixing and maintaining local infrastructure.

Benali is a Highbury based Green member.

He's worked on the official Remain and as a campaginer for a health charity.

Benali has led a variety of successful LGBT+ campaigns. Fighting the return of Section 28 in schools and the dealth penalty in Brunei.

Jon has lived in Islington for the past 19 years and worked in the borough for most of that time. He now works as Chief Executive of a national children's charity.

Jon is a campaigner for affordable housing and safer streets and works to promote urban green spaces and allotments.

A stronger, fairer, more hopeful Islington really is possible if we work together.

As Councillor, I promise to work for the benefit of all Hillrise residents - not for a political party.

My Commitment is Clear:

Challenging the system to deliver real change for local people. We can all see how austerity, privatisation, and top-down politics have eroded trust and harmed ordinary people.

I am not afraid to name the source of the problem, successive governments in Westminster which have cut funding while giving councils even more responsibilities.  This has to stop and we need to explain this reality to our neighbours and unite them in order to fight back. If elected as your local councillor, I will fight any budget that proposes further cuts and stand  with the people most affected.

Let's make Islington work for the many, not the few.

Warm greetings to one and all,

I'm proudly standing, as a candidate representing the Hillrise ward. Because, I firmly believe, that through building a bond with you, the members of the community.

This can in-turn, bolster the connection between political figures, and members of the community. Where, faith is reignited into the community. In which, I seek to develop social-cohesion within our community.

Where your voice is heard, and acknowledged within the political arena. Moreover, striving to bring forth social cohesion. Whereby, community spirit is channeled throughout the atmosphere.

Striving to decrease crime rates, where we all can feel safe, and walk confidently within our local community.

Claire has lived in Holloway – on the same estate currently called ‘Hyde Village’ for 32 years and in Islington Borough for 42 years. She lives with her partner and has a 32 year old daughter who lives in Berlin. She is semi-retired and with a 4-hour a week job working for a peace networking organisation based above Housmans Bookshop in Kings Cross.

Leon has lived in London for 24 years, including 12 years in Islington on the border of the Tollington and Finsbury Park wards. His connection to the area goes back his whole life through his dad.

Leon is standing as a candidate because he believes his experience as a chartered structural engineer can make a positive difference for Islington. He is used to solving complex problems and wants to use that skill to make the most of the council’s limited budget and help make the borough a good place to live for everyone.

Leon believes decisions should not happen behind closed doors and that local people must be empowered. He supports participatory democracy, including participatory budgeting, so residents have a meaningful role in shaping their community.

Leon thinks the council should depend less on outsourcing for inspections, repairs and improvement works in council housing. Bringing more of this work in‑house would mean quicker, better‑quality repairs, improved living conditions for tenants and lower service charges for leaseholders.

Leon backs liveable neighbourhoods that reduce traffic and pollution, make streets safer and quieter, and open space for cycling, walking and community use. He has joined the local residents’ group to raise awareness and build support.

I am an Islington resident of over 15 years, with a family history in the Irish community of Junction Ward. I am not from a traditional political background, so my motivation comes purely from being a resident myself and witnessing first hand the problems that impact our communities. I am standing to represent my fellow residents and give them a voice in Islington Town Hall. I am also very proud to be part of a community that stands so strongly in solidarity with the Palestinian people, and to have signed Islington PSC's Candidate Pledge for Palestine.

My background is in arts education, and I have worked locally for many years, including mentoring at the National Youth Theatre on Holloway Road, helping young people build confidence, find their voice, and develop skills for life. I have seen first hand the ways in which art changes and enriches lives, particularly for those struggling elsewhere in their lives with housing, health, or access to education. Cultural spaces are something that everyone should have access to and it is heartening to hear from Junction residents that there is already lots of support for the development of an arts centre in Archway.

In Summer 2024, I replied to a call out for volunteers to help elect Jeremy Corbyn as an Independent MP. I had always voted for Jeremy, and felt it my duty to help ensure his victory. I will never forget how nervous I was walking into his office, only to find that I was fully embraced by the whole team. Following Jeremy’s election victory, I became involved in Jeremy’s regular community meetings, working diligently behind the scenes to co-organise and facilitate on topics affecting local residents, such as housing conditions, health and worker’s rights. In Islington, we are lucky to have so many incredible activists, and I have been proud to support local campaigns, including collaborating with my incredible colleague Cllr. Ilkay Cinko-Oner to set up a desperately needed Parents & Carers Union, and supporting S

I’m a local resident committed to strong education where no child is left behind, full SEND and EHCP support, safe and affordable housing, reliable services, and putting our community’s needs first in every decision.

My name is Pat Prendergast. I have lived in Islington for the past 2 years while studying history at University at UCL.

Despite my short time here in Islington I’ve been committed to helping in the community, volunteering for the Islington North People’s forum, helping with Acorn to oppose unfair evictions, getting involved in our Your Party Proto-Branch, and joining our already well established activism networks. I have met so many kind, knowledgeable and committed people who have helped me find a home here and welcomed me into this community.

At my university I have organised a Your Party society to bring together students across UCL and other London Universities, join the fight for improved workers rights in UCL, and support our BDS movement against UCL’s complicity in genocide. I did this in the hopes of bringing the welcoming community spirit in Islington to other students across London.
Standing as a councillor means supporting the community. I will fight for improved housing, a reliable and supported healthcare, and better support for our young people. But more than that I will be responsible and accountable to our community - bridging the gap between politics and the people who it affects.

I have seen worrying apathy in students and young people surrounding politics, in which it is a separate, unchangeable world for older out of touch people to make all the decisions. I want to bring local politics that is local and community based, ensure young people get their voices heard, and
ultimately do some good and give back to the borough that has welcomed, inspired and uplifted me for these past 2 years.

I am incredibly excited for this election and whatever follows and cannot wait to keep meeting and supporting the many Islingtonians that make this an incredibly special place.

My name is Rasan. I grew up in North London and now live in Laycock, working across local GP practices. I’m Kurdish, and my family came to Britain when I was a baby after fleeing conflict in the Middle East. That has shaped how I think about community, security, and the importance of feeling supported where you live. In my work, I spend a lot of time talking to people about their health, and you quickly see how often the same issues come up: housing, stress, money, isolation, and the environment around us. It’s a big part of why I care about local politics. Health isn’t just what happens in a clinic. It’s shaped by the conditions we live in.

I’m standing because I want an Islington where people can live well, with strong local services, decent housing, and communities that feel supported. I’ll back practical local action that improves health, fairness, and quality of life.

Voting for me will give you a voice. Standing up for what is right and just. Protecting the future. Making Britain great again by focusing on four key issues that affect us all:

> Making our education system the best in the world; > Ending the inequalities in the NHS to provide the best health care for all; > Taking action on the cost of living crisis that has hit many of us in Islington by ensuring fair pay and affordable housing; > Promoting a safer and more sustainable future with more renewable energy and safer streets.

Christopher has lived for 26 years in St Mary's & StJames' since 2000. He was influenced by his step father a long time Labour Councillor in Lewisham to become active locally. As a new resident in 2001 he secured funding for Cross street parking improvements and a new zebra crossing. He is an architect specialising in retrofitting homes for low energy / low carbon and new passive houses. He was course leader for Technical Studies, MA Architecture, Royal College of Art for 8 years.

After the UK declared Climate Emergency 2019, he campaigned for Islington to rethink local planning to encourage innovation. He realized that 50% of the borough is within conservation areas and without heritage climate retrofit the borough would not be able to reduce resident’s energy and fuel costs. During covid he researched one Islington Conservation Area to see where reductions could be made. He collaborated with and wrote the heritage section of the influential UK 2021 ‘Climate Emergency Retrofit Guide’ by LETI.

Christopher campaigned with the Islington Environmental Alliance and the Green Conservation Petition which gathered 2,221 signatures forcing a debate in council for proactive heritage climate retrofit in July 2023. During Covid his Islington conservation area research became a toolkit for action. His drafts were not well received by Islington planners, but the work was published in March 2023 as, "Climate Emergency Conservation Area Toolkit- England". Launched at several trade shows, 500 hard copies sold out (more pdfs) reaching councils, architects, and planners across the country. These multiple resident-led campaigns pressured, Islington to renew and fund work on the overdue "Climate Action SPD" and the "Retrofit Handbook" published in September 2025. Christopher supplied his book to the council and extensive edits to the planning policy team to radically change the early drafts and make sure the new Islington documents would be current.

Christopher said, "I bring passio

Toby North – a charity campaigner, proud trade unionist, school governor and committed local organiser – will be Islington Labour’s candidate in St Peter’s on May 6th. Toby works in campaigns for a children’s charity, putting pressure on local and national governments to improve lives and reduce child poverty. Since moving to the borough, Toby has served as campaigns coordinator for both Islington constituencies and as a ward organiser for two branches, leading on campaigns to protect our NHS build more genuinely affordable homes for local people.
from: https://www.islington-labour.org.uk/bashir-angelo-toby-for-islington/

Alex has lived in Caledonian ward for over thirty years, and has been a candidate in the last three local elections, after joining the Green Party in 2005.

He wants to make the council more accountable to local people, and to mitigate the effects of government cuts on local services.

John has lived 18 years in Islington since 2008, and is an active contributor to the Tenants and Residents Association on the multicultural estate where he has lived since 2009. He joined the Green Party in 2023, and was elected Treasurer of the Islington Green Party in September 2025.

John said,”I joined the Green party in frustration at the stance of the major parties over many issues, particularly Gaza, and fully support the strong stance taken by party leadership and conference on the need for all countries, even our allies, to uphold international law. In my work as a specialist in conflict casualty recording, I see the damage being done by Britain’s support of illegal US and Israel actions in the Middle East, actions which have direct consequences for the people living and working in the streets of Islington.”

John would bring to the council his concern for ethical divestment and making Islington a thriving community safe and welcoming for all.

Martin Lunnon, Liberal Democrat candidate for St Peter's and Canalside Ward, Islington, 2026
https://www.islingtonlibdem.com/ 
I'm a retired actuary, with 30 years' experience in the public sector, and subsequent service on the finance sub-committee of the board of Amnesty International UK. This has given me a wealth of experience of managing risk and financial projections. As such I feel I could contribute a lot to Islington Council as it faces ever greater demands on its services with limited financial resources.
I've a strong interest in planning, urbanism and transport. As someone who currently faces some mobility challenges, I'm concerned about reliable accessible public transport, and about having good pavements so that people who face difficulties getting around can easily access bus-stops and stations.
I'm passionate about human rights and justice, and, as well as Amnesty International, I have a long history of volunteering with charities supporting asylum-seekers and refugees and people living with HIV and AIDS.
Finally, I love Islington. I lived here for 35 years, almost all my adult life, for most of that time in St Peter's ward. I'm training to be an Islington tour guide, and volunteer with a local history group. It would be an honour to serve such a beautiful and vibrant area as a councillor.

Trade union activist Mick Gilgunn has been selected as Islington Labour’s candidate to contest the Tollington by-election on Thursday 1st July.

A lifelong activist and advocate of workers’ rights, Mick has played his part in campaigns from defending Council housing and the Whittington Hospital through to workplace activism as a member of Unite the Union and Chair of Islington Trade Union Council.

Mick works in a maintenance department at a London university and has lived in Islington for over two decades. He is passionately committed to the rights of private renters, reducing health inequalities, and tackling the climate crisis.

Mick Gilgunn, Islington Labour’s candidate for the Tollington by-election, said:

“I am honoured to have been given the opportunity to stand for election and represent our wonderful community here in Tollington. Our borough is a fantastic place to live and work, but the impact of Tory austerity leaves us with so many challenges in the years ahead – from cutting crime to building the affordable homes we so urgently need.

“If elected, I will work hard to support the Council’s bold action to protect the environment with more school streets and a new generation of high-quality, skilled, green jobs for our young people. I’ll also do everything in my power to save our NHS, campaigning against the privatisation of GP surgeries and defending local services.

“I’m so looking forward to working with Tollington’s fantastic Councillors – Anjna Khurana and Flora Williamson – and the whole Islington Labour team to help make our borough the best place to grow up and grow old in.”

The 1st July poll will take place following the resignation of Richard Watts as ward Councillor. Residents wishing to register for a postal vote can do so here.

Sophia has lived in Tufnell Park all her life, and is standing because she genuinely loves her area and enjoys helping people; a casework win is a proud moment. She comes from a working-class background and wants to show people that you do not need to come from privilege to enter politics.

Sophia says “The Green Party is the only group that has the environment at its core. Nature will not survive without breathable air and predictable temperatures. I’ve been a member for 5.5 years.”

Sophia used to work in the Community Engagement Team at The British Library, so she’s used to communicating with residents about issues personally impacting them. “For the past year and a half I’ve been door knocking and listening to the concerns of residents, then raising it through our Green councillors to get action. My main priority is being a reliable source of contact that people can come to; someone who listens and resolves. Areas of interest to me are social housing, both council and housing association properties, and processes around recycling and waste management.”

Devon was just 124 votes away from becoming the first Green Councillor in Tufnell Park in 2022. She’s worked and volunteered in community centres and programmes across Islington over the last 7 years, and her priority would be to make sure that residents can easily access the support they need.

“Tufnell Park is my home. I want to work with you to make sure that the day-to-day things that would make your life easier are being done by the council”, Devon said. “Actions speak louder than words — that’s why I’ve reported over 100 potholes this year. When I tell you I want Tufnell Park to be a safer, better place to live, you can see we’re already doing the hard work”.

Much of her local paid and voluntary work has enabled her to build deep relationships with residents over many years, listening with patience and respect to the range of diverse viewpoints that make up our community. She would continue to fight for all of your everyday problems like bins and drains to quality of life issues like damp and mould.

Sheridan is a former software engineer turned ecological economist, who has lived in Islington since 2020. She works on developing policy and education programmes to show how we can build a different kind of economy that actually works for people and planet.

“People all over Islington are seeing their council tax bills and business rates rise at an alarming rate, and yet services are being cut all over the place,” Sheridan said. “So much of the blame lies with the Labour government refusing to restore critical funding. We’ll work with Green councillors all over London to expose this dereliction of duty and insist the government step up to do what’s needed.”

She’s also running to help change how decisions are made in Tufnell Park, to ensure residents have a stronger say on issues affecting their lives, with a focus on participatory budgeting and collective decision-making. “We’ll work to ensure that people aren’t just having things done to them — we’ve been knocking on doors for years now, and will keep doing that so everyone knows their opinion and contributions are truly needed and valued.”

As the lead pastor of a Christian church in Islington (The Angel Church) and as a resident of this borough for more than a decade I am well aware of the many positives and negatives of life in our borough. There is no other place in this city or country where I would rather be. I am deeply invested in seeing the lives of those around me bettered and truly want to see each individual in Islington, no matter their age, stage, or ability flourish. This is, I hope, well evidenced in my church and community work which spans interaction with every type of person with every type of need you can imagine.

Local government plats a very important role in helping achieve the best for each individual person as part of the larger collective of our city and of our nation. I am not in the business of toxic negativity that is consuming so much of our politics across the political spectrum. I have no desire to impugn or discredit any of our public servants in the tasks they have undertaken, I am sure to the best of their ability within their capacity. I do know and believe we could do better. I believe the people of Islington could be better looked after and I believe the people of Tufnell Park could have better representation. I will not make promises I may not be able keep due to democratic procedures or beurocratic red tape, but I can promise my complete commitment and attentiveness in representing the people of Tufnell Park fairly and well.

I hope to receive your vote.
